spec_match.rationale: The page now feature-detects and calls the known SPC availability entry points, including PaymentRequest.securePaymentConfirmationAvailability(), rather than only narrating the branch.

interactive.rationale: Probe target, fallback scenario, merchant preference, probe/reset buttons, telemetry, and route decision output all change visible behavior.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept page lede restates and delivers on the framing used in the feature index card.

edge_cases.rationale: Page surfaces multiple failure / unsupported / edge-case branches in the UI, not just the happy path.

references.rationale: The see-also section now cites chromestatus.com/feature/5165040614768640 and the SPC availability spec discussion.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables throughout, no raw hex of note, and button overrides keep background + color paired (CLAUDE.md rules 5 and 6).

summary: The router-vs-fallback page now clears the major reference gap and the API-surface critique: it calls real availability probes where present, handles missing/error branches, and shows the resulting checkout route decision.
